summary: CONSTITUTIONAL CHECKS: ALL 4 PASS — Cantonal satisfied (identity_resolution_errors is cross-cutting infrastructure ERROR table, not a sub-hub; existing CT/DOL/Blog/People CANONICAL+ERROR pairs untouched), Registry-First (CTB pre-registration Section 0 before CREATE TABLE), Sovereign ID (no minting — resolves existing identities only), No sideways calls (reads outreach.outreach + cl.company_identity for legitimate identity lookup, writes only to own schema). ENVELOPE: PASS — all 4 fields match (change_type=feature, architectural_flag=false, doctrine_version=2.8.0). SCOPE: PASS — 2 modified paths within migrations/ and tests/. No protected assets touched. EXECUTION: PASS — all 6 acceptance criteria met. identity_resolution_errors created as append-only ERROR with column-comparison guard trigger (only resolved_at updatable) + delete block trigger + CHECK constraints on resolution_stage and error_type. fn_resolve_identity implements 4 resolution paths (outreach_id > sovereign_id > domain > ein) with hard-fail on ambiguity/missing_anchor/invalid_input/not_found, deterministic error logging with reason payload. identity_resolver role with EXECUTE-only, REVOKE ALL on outreach.outreach + cl.company_identity + error table. 14 enforcement tests across 6 categories. OBSERVATIONS (non-blocking, verify before deployment): (1) Resolver references outreach.outreach.sovereign_id but Phase 2A test and CLAUDE.md use sovereign_company_id — column name must be verified against live schema. (2) Paths 3-4 query outreach.outreach.domain and outreach.outreach.ein which may not exist on that table (typically on company_target and dol respectively) — verify live schema.
